details: Chai-as-patched@7.0.6 is a combosquat of chai-as-promised and a remote-code-execution dropper. index.js spawns a detached, unref'd background node process running lib/initializeCaller.js, which base64-decodes a URL (hxxps://tomato-brunhilda-40[.]tiiny[.]site/index.json), fetches it via axios, and executes the response via new Function.constructor("require", response) — giving full Node module access. The stealth detached spawn and remotely-fetched code execution make this a dropper that can deliver arbitrary attacker payloads. chai-as-patched@7.0.6 is a combosquat dropper. index.js spawns a detached child process running lib/initializeCaller.js, which decodes a base64 URL (hxxps://tomato-brunhilda-40[.]tiiny[.]site/index.json), fetches remote code via axios, and executes it via new Function.constructor("require", response) — arbitrary remote code execution that auto-runs on require(). chai-as-patched@7.0.6 is a combosquat of chai-as-promised. lib/initializeCaller.js executes arbitrary code fetched from hxxps://tomato-brunhilda-40[.]tiiny[.]site/index.json (base64-decoded from a fake DEV_API_KEY) via new Function.constructor("require", response). index.js spawns this payload as a detached child process (detached:true, stdio:ignore, unref) for stealth. This is a remote-code-execution dropper. chai-as-patched@7.0.6 is a combosquat (mimics chai-as-promised) and an RCE dropper. On require() it spawns a detached child process running lib/initializeCaller.js, which base64-decodes an attacker-controlled URL (hxxps://tomato-brunhilda-40[.]tiiny[.]site/index.json), fetches remote content via axios, and executes it as JS via new Function("require", response) — arbitrary remote code execution. The attacker controls the endpoint and can deliver any payload.
